channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Eastern Cape MEC warns that ignoring undocumented immigrant issue could cause turmoil in South Africa
item.description: Eastern Cape Education MEC, Fundile Gade, has voiced concerns over the rising issue of undocumented immigrants in South Africa. Gade argues that the government’s failure to address this crisis could l...

item.title: Finance Bill 2026 Could Raise Taxes for KQ and Digital Services
item.description: The proposed Finance Bill 2026 in Kenya is set to remove key tax exemptions for Kenya Airways (KQ) and broaden the taxation scope across digital payments and software services. Specifically, the bill ...

item.title: Preparing Your Outdoor Space for the May Long Weekend
item.description: As the unofficial start of patio season in Canada approaches from May 2-4, homeowners are reminded that after months of winter dormancy, outdoor spaces need proper maintenance before they are ready fo...

item.title: Jewish Australians give testimony on rising antisemitism at Royal Commission
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion commenced its first day of public hearings in Sydney, presided over by former High Court judge Virginia Bell. The focus is on defining antisemi...

item.title: Albert Ojwang’s family struggles despite earlier financial support and unfulfilled promises
item.description: The family of the late blogger and teacher Albert Ojwang has spoken out nearly one year after his passing, revealing that they are still facing serious financial hardship despite receiving millions in...

item.title: Kenya and Tanzania leaders meet to strengthen bilateral cooperation
item.description: Today, May 4, 2026, President William Samoei Ruto of Kenya is arriving in Tanzania for a two-day official visit. The main goal of this visit is to strengthen the already close relations between Tanzan...

item.title: Femi Fani-Kayode Speaks on the Treatment of Nigerians in South Africa
item.description: Dis na one serious message from Femi Fani-Kayode wey dey address how some Nigerians dey suffer for South Africa. E draw comparison with Julius Caesar story to show betrayal and pain wey e dey feel as ...

item.title: Endangered North Atlantic right whales record 23 calves in strongest calving season since 2009
item.description: The latest calving season for North Atlantic right whales has brought a rare piece of positive news for the critically endangered species. Between mid-November and mid-April, researchers recorded 23 n...

item.title: Police Intercept Bus Carrying Ammunition and Government Items on Thika-Garissa Highway
item.description: Kenya police, together with a multi-agency security team, intercepted a passenger bus transporting ammunition and government stores along the Thika-Garissa Highway. The bus, operated by Buscar Sacco, ...

item.title: Examining the Impact and Fairness of AI Road Safety Cameras in Australia
item.description: AI-powered road safety cameras have been widely introduced across Australia, issuing a substantial number of fines to drivers. Since their rollout, around 184,000 fines have been issued in Western Aus...

item.title: Langara College evaluates temporary pause of journalism program amid declining enrolment
item.description: Langara College in Vancouver is contemplating a temporary pause in its journalism program, causing concern among students, faculty, and the broader media community. The college cites declining enrolme...

item.title: Welsh Greens could hold balance of power as Plaid Cymru challenges Labour dominance
item.description: The Welsh Green Party is positioning itself as a potential kingmaker in the upcoming Senedd elections, as Plaid Cymru appears poised to end nearly three decades of Labour dominance. Green activists fr...

item.title: Four Suspects Arrested for Attempted Jet Fuel Theft at Wilson Airport
item.description: Nairobi detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested four individuals after intercepting a suspicious operation at Wilson Airport where they tried to siphon jet fuel....

item.title: Centre to Provide Lifelong Diabetes Screening and Care for All Children Under RBSK
item.description: In a significant policy development, the Indian Government has announced that all children from birth up to 18 years will be screened for diabetes under an expanded Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (R...

item.title: Top Nigerian News Stories for Monday, 4th May, 2026
item.description: Good morning Naija. Today na the roundup of major headlines wey dey make waves across di country. Peter Obi and Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso don officially join Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), collect thei...

item.title: Family protests demanding death penalty for accused in 3-year-old girl's rape and murder in Pune
item.description: A serious incident shook the Bhor-Rajgad region of Pune district, Maharashtra, after the rape and murder of a three-year-old girl. Her family refused to meet any politicians until the 65-year-old accu...

item.title: Three-Vehicle Collision Reported on Ortigas Flyover in Mandaluyong
item.description: Earlier today, three vehicles were involved in a chain collision on the Ortigas Flyover in Mandaluyong City. The incident occurred during morning hours, causing temporary traffic congestion in the are...

item.title: 22 Iranian Crew Members on Seized Ship Evacuated to Pakistan for Repatriation
item.description: The Foreign Office of Pakistan confirmed that 22 crew members of the Iranian container ship 'MV Touska', which was seized by US forces for allegedly violating the US blockade on Iranian ports, have be...

item.title: Proteas Women Secure Convincing Victory Over Bangladesh
item.description: On May 4 in South African sport history, several notable events are remembered. In 1947, golf legend Bobby Locke became the first South African to claim a tournament victory in the US, winning the Car...

item.title: David Attenborough Reflects on Iconic Wildlife Moments in 'Making Life On Earth'
item.description: In 'Making Life On Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure', Sir David Attenborough, who will soon celebrate his centenary, revisits defining moments from his illustrious career. The programme highli...
